rulings.Case Title: KARENA APPLE FENG ET AL VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR FRIDAY, MAR-06-2026. LINE 13. 2-PETITIONERS KARENA FENG, and LILIAN FENG'S Motion For Writ Of Mandate. Hearing required. The tentative ruling posted on February 26 stated: Petitioners' petition for writ of mandate is denied without prejudice to being re-noticed. At the time the petitioners noticed their petition for hearing the DMV's motion for judgment on the pleadings was pending and thus the notice requesting that the petition be heard on its merits is premature and not ripe. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.=(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KARENA APPLE FENG ET AL VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR FRIDAY, MAR-06-2026. LINE 13. 1-RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES' MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. Hearing required. The tentative ruling posted on February 26 stated: DMV's motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied. The petition for writ of mandate shows that the claims alleged therein are filed per CCP 1085, not 1094.5, and thus the claims are not time-barred. The petition does not show on its face that Karena's claim is barred by laches or that petitioners have an adequate legal remedy. DMV's argument in its reply brief that the petition seeks to compel a discretionary act that is not cognizable per 1085 is not considered because it was not addressed in the moving papers. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: TWANDA BAILEY VS. SAN FRANCISCO DISTRICT ATTORNEY'S OFFICE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Leave To Take Further Deposition Of Plaintiff And To Compel Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR FRIDAY, MAR-06-2026. LINE 1. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Leave To Take Further Deposition Of Plaintiff And To Compel Deposition. Defendant City and County's motion to take further deposition of plaintiff Twanda Bailey is granted. No later than April 3, 2026 Bailey must submit to a further session of her deposition of up to four hours in length excluding any breaks that she wishes to take. The City has shown good cause for an additional session of Bailey's deposition. Neither already served written discovery nor a deposition on written questions is a satisfactory substitute for an oral deposition. The City's questions are limited to matters occurring after Bailey's prior deposition. Bailey is entitled to any reasonable accommodation, including more frequent and longer breaks than usual, that she requests due to her medical condition. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: JONATHON SALERNO VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR FRIDAY, MAR-06-2026. LINE 4. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary adjudication of the first, third, fourth, sixth, eighth and ninth causes of action is denied as to all six causes of action. Plaintiff Jonathon Salerno has presented sufficient evidence to create a triable dispute as to each of the six causes of action. As to the first cause of action for disability discrimination, the third cause of action for FEHA retaliation, and the sixth cause of action for failure to prevent discrimination, harassment or retaliation, there are triable issues whether the reassignment of Salerno from acting senior stationary engineer to stationary engineer is an adverse action and was motivated by Salerno's disability and his request to accommodate that disability. As to the fourth cause of action for disability harassment, there are triable issues whether the conduct of co-worker Ruedell Walker was motivated by Salerno's disability and created a hostile work environment. As to the eighth cause of action for failure to accommodate and the ninth cause of action for failure to engage in an interactive process, there are triable issues whether, in revoking Salerno's permission to have an emotional support animal and in reassigning him from acting senior stationary engineer to stationary engineer, the City failed to reasonably accommodate Salerno's disability and failed to act in good faith in an interactive process regarding Salerno's disability. In making the above rulings, the court relied only on admissible evidence. Salerno is required to submit a proposed order consistent with the requirements of CCP 437c. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: SARKIS GEGAMYAN VS. ATLASSIAN US, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Defendants Atlassian Usa Inc Notice Of Taking Continued Deposition Of Plaintiff Sarkis Gegamyan
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR FRIDAY, MAR-06-2026. LINE 17. PLAINTIFF SARKIS GEGAMYAN'S, AN INDIVIDUAL, Motion To Quash Defendants Atlassian Usa Inc Notice Of Taking Continued Deposition Of Plaintiff Sarkis Gegamyan. Hearing required. The tentative ruling posted on February 27 stated: Plaintiff Sarkis Gegamyan's motion to quash the notice of taking his continued deposition is denied. Plaintiff failed to meet and confer prior to filing this motion. Even more significantly, page 277 of the transcript of the first session of his deposition shows that his then attorney agreed to additional "days" of his deposition. Nor does plaintiff provide any support for any cognizable reason to avoid a further session of his deposition. rulings.Case Title: JESSICA BLACK VS. GOLDEN GATE BRIDGE HIGHWAY TRANSPORTATION DISTRICT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Interrogatories, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR FRIDAY, MAR-06-2026. LINE 8. 1-PLAINTIFF JESSICA BLACK'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Interrogatories, Set One. Continued to March 17, 2026 to give the parties an opportunity to meet and confer in an effort to resolve all or some of the issues raised by the motion. The parties are required to fully meet and confer as to each issue raised by the motion no later than 6pm on March 12, 2026 in person or by video platform. As non-binding guidance for the parties, the court states that: 1) stipulated protective orders are generally a good way to resolve discovery disputes regarding assertedly sensitive and confidential information; 2) a privilege log is usually required for responsive information withheld on the grounds of privilege; 3) in employment cases general form interrogatories are often of little or no value and it is better to use employment form interrogatories; 4) discovery of insurance information is not unlimited, but is usually tied to what will assist the parties in engaging in settlement efforts; and 5) discovery of "me too" information is not unlimited, but is usually tied to the same or similar circumstances, people involved, and locations as as the basis for plaintiff's claims. Counsel for plaintiff Jessica Black is required to send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org no later than 4pm on March 16, 2026 stating, without argument, whether all issues raised by the motion have been resolved by the parties and, if not, identifying what issues need to be resolved by the court. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.=(301/HEK)

As non-binding guidance for the parties, the court states that: 1) stipulated protective orders are generally a good way to resolve discovery disputes regarding assertedly sensitive and confidential information; 2) a privilege log is usually required for responsive information withheld on the grounds of privilege; 3) in employment cases general form interrogatories are often of little or no value and it is better to use employment form interrogatories; 4) discovery of insurance information is not unlimited, but is usually tied to what will assist the parties in engaging in settlement efforts; and 5) discovery of "me too" information is not unlimited, but is usually tied to the same or similar circumstances, people involved, and locations as as the basis for plaintiff's claims. Counsel for plaintiff Jessica Black is required to send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org no later than 4pm on March 16, 2026 stating, without argument, whether all issues raised by the motion have been resolved by the parties and, if not, identifying what issues need to be resolved by the court. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.=(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA HARRINGTON ET AL VS. ALEJANDRA GARCIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, To Deem All Objections Waived, And For Monetary Sanctions From Plaintiff Linda Harrington And Plaintiffs Counsel Of Record
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR FRIDAY, MAR-06-2026. LINE 18. DEFENDANT ALEJANDRA GARCIA'S Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, To Deem All Objections Waived, And For Monetary Sanctions From Plaintiff Linda Harrington And Plaintiffs Counsel Of Record. Hearing required. The tentative ruling posted on February 27 stated: Defendant Alejandra Garcia's motion to compel responses to form interrogatories and for monetary sanctions is granted in part. No later than March 5, 2026 plaintiff Linda Harrington must serve verified code-complaint responses to the form interrogatories served on her and the only objections she may assert are privilege and work product. No later than March 19, 2026 plaintiff must pay $1,000 in reasonable monetary sanctions to Garcia. In the exercise of its discretion, the court will not entertain live evidence on this motion. Even if plaintiff was not able to respond to the interrogatories on a timely basis, it was her obligation to notify Garcia on a timely basis that she was unable to do so, which she did not. If the responses are served by March 5, it would be equitable to preclude plaintiff from asserting any privilege and work product objections she may have, but nor is it equitable to allow plaintiff to assert other objections. For a simple motion, Garcia's request for $3,100 in monetary sanctions is excessive. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.=(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LESLEY GISELLE HOWARD VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Employment Law
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR FRIDAY, MAR-06-2026. LINE 19. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Employment Law. Hearing required. The tentative ruling posted on February 27 stated: Defendant City and County of San Francisco' motion for sanction for failure to comply with discovery order is granted in part. Plaintiff is precluded from introducing any evidence or making any argument at trial as to any subject that is covered by employment law form interrogatories 201.3, 203.1, 207.2 and 213.2 that she did not provide a response enabling the City to understand and defend against. This issue sanction sufficiently protects the City from being surprised at trial and accepts the representation by plaintiff that she has fully responded to the interrogatories. Monetary sanctions are denied because the plaintiff did not act without substantial justification in opposing this motion.
